title: Self-Controlled Cohort Analysis
description: Self-Controlled Cohort (SCC) analysis estimates the association between a drug exposure and an outcome by comparing event rates during exposed and unexposed periods within the same patients. This design controls for between-person confounding by using each patient as their own control.
tips: Define your target drug and outcome precisely — the SCC design is sensitive to temporal overlap between exposure and outcome windows.
The unexposed period is defined as time before first exposure or after washout — configure these windows carefully.
Incidence rate ratios greater than 1.0 suggest a positive association between the exposure and outcome.
SCC is particularly well-suited for acute outcomes that occur shortly after drug initiation.
Run the analysis on multiple data sources to assess consistency across populations before drawing conclusions.
